checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=55,807,103 (2014-12-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=49,069,668 (2013-12-31), a ~13.7% increase
SalesRevenueNet=286,242,169 (FY2014) vs SalesRevenueNet=244,870,731 (FY2013), a ~16.9% increase - revenue growth exceeded AR growth
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=61,650,439 (2014-09-30) vs 60,620,981 (2013-09-30) (+1.7%); SalesRevenueNet=72,729,678 (Q3-2014) vs 70,176,216 (Q3-2013) (+3.6%) - AR grew slower than revenue

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=30,498,802 (2012-12-31)
IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=36,807,099 (2013-12-31), a ~20.7% one-year increase
SalesRevenueNet=228,537,050 (FY2012) vs SalesRevenueNet=244,870,731 (FY2013), only a ~7.2% increase, well below the intangibles growth rate

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=7,372,756 (FY2013)
NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-2,453,218 (FY2013)
NetIncomeLoss=8,854,765 (FY2012); NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=17,993,553 (FY2012), showing OCF far exceeding NI the prior year, unlike FY2013

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueNet=228,537,050 (FY2012); SalesRevenueNet=244,870,731 (FY2013), only a ~7.2% increase, below inventory growth

GrossProfit=80,505,977 (FY2012) on SalesRevenueNet=228,537,050 = 35.2% margin; GrossProfit=83,542,451 (FY2013) on SalesRevenueNet=244,870,731 = 34.1% margin, a declining trend continuing to 33.7% in FY2014

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=14,055,201 (FY2010); 6,529,558 (FY2011); 17,993,553 (FY2012); -2,453,218 (FY2013); 12,980,048 (FY2014) - highly volatile including a negative year

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueNet=239,968,770 (FY2011) growing to SalesRevenueNet=286,242,169 (FY2014), a ~19% increase while accrued liabilities declined

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-Q/A filing_date=2012-08-06 (restating period ended 2011-06-30, filed over a year after the original)
10-Q/A filing_date=2012-10-31 (restating period ended 2011-09-30)
10-K/A filing_date=2013-03-05, one day after original 10-K filed 2013-03-04

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans instant/duration facts from 2008-12-31 through 2015-06-30 across Assets, Liabilities, Revenue, COGS, Inventory, AR, Cash Flow, and Equity concepts, but excludes allowance for doubtful accounts, tax detail, segment data, and restatement rationale

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible aggressive channel loading / inventory build reflected in FY2013 where reported net income remained positive while operating cash flow turned negative, driven by inventory growing (~16.4%) faster than revenue (~7.2%) and gross margin compression - consistent with inventory not being written down/reserved adequately or revenue being recognized ahead of cash collection.

Potential earnings smoothing via under-accrual of liabilities/expenses (e.g., warranty, incentive comp, or other reserves) to dampen the effect of revenue/cash-flow volatility on reported net income, evidenced by an unusually flat-to-declining accrued liabilities balance despite ~19% revenue growth from 2011-2014.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss ranged narrowly between 7,372,756 and 9,845,298 (FY2011-FY2014) despite revenue swings of -5% to +17% YoY

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Prior-period misclassification or error correction reflected in two 10-Q/A restatements (FY2011 Q2 and Q3) and a same-week 10-K/A after the FY2012 10-K, combined with a discrete ~$6.3M step-up in intangible assets in FY2013 (from $30.5M to $36.8M) disproportionate to revenue growth, suggesting possible misallocation of purchase-price or capitalized costs around this period.

top_signals: FY2013 positive net income ($7.37M) alongside negative operating cash flow (-$2.45M), a large one-year divergence
Inventory growth (+16.4% in FY2013) outpacing revenue growth (+7.2%) with concurrent gross margin compression (36.8%->33.7% over FY2011-FY2014)
Unusually smooth net income ($7.4M-$9.8M range) versus highly volatile revenue (-5% to +17% YoY) and operating cash flow (-$2.45M to +$18M)
Flat-to-declining accrued liabilities relative to ~19% revenue growth 2011-2014, suggesting possible under-accrual
Cluster of restatement-related filings: two FY2011 10-Q/A amendments and a 10-K/A filed the day after the original FY2012 10-K
